Cost Reduction Idea:
Redesign the current front logo assembly making the mentioned changes,
1. Remove the Logo Holder (Black)
2. Instead of chrome plating the Logo Holding Bracket, finish it in Black and snap-fit the logo M and G onto this
3. Introduce a new chrome-plated edge trim to complete the design
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing,
1. The Per Kg cost of PC-ABS = INR 311.17
2. The Per Kg cost of ABS = INR 165.45
3. The Per Sq.M cost of chrome plating = INR 129.14
4. The weight saved de-contenting the holder trim = 39.16g
5. The cost saved = INR 12.18
6. The estimated weight of the proposed edge trim = 15g
7. The estimated cost = INR 2.48
8. The estimated surface area = 0.00249755 m2
9. The Chrome Plating cost = INR 0.32

10. The estimated weight saved = 39.16 - 15 = 24.16g
11. The estimated cost saved = 12.18 - (2.48 + 0.32) = 12.18 - 2.8 = INR 9.38
Proposal:
Redesign the current front logo assembly making the mentioned changes,
1. Remove the Logo Holder (Black)
2. Instead of chrome plating the Logo Holding Bracket, finish it in Black and snap-fit the logo M and G onto this
3. Introduce a new chrome-plated edge trim to complete the design

Cost Reduction Idea: Replace the 16-pin connector set used on the IRVM assembly with an 8-pin connector set.
Way Forward: Confirm on 10 pin connector availability with supplier (similar to Kia)
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing.
1. Current weight of the connector set - 8 + 6 = 14g (assumed)
2. The estimated weight of the proposed set - 5 + 4 = 9g (assumed)
3. Current cost of the connector set - 20 + 20 = INR 40 (assumed)
4. The estimated cost of the proposed set  - 12 + 12 = INR 24 (assumed)

5. The weight saved on the replacement = 14 - 9 = 5g
6. The cost saved on the replacement = 40 - 24 = INR 16
Proposal: Replace the 16-pin connector set used on the IRVM assembly with an 8-pin connector set.
Imp: Confirm on 10 pin connector availability with supplier (similar to Kia)

Cost Reduction Idea: Replace Brass with Steel in the manufacture of the Main Terminal Connector Bolt within the Engine-Bay Fusebox Assembly of MGI
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing,
1. The per Kg rate of Brass - INR 740
2. The per Kg rate of Steel - INR 160
3. The current weight of the bolt = 28g
4. The estimated weight of the proposed bolt = 24.72g
5. The estimated weight saved = 28 - 24.72 = 3.28g
6. The current cost of the bolt = INR 20.72
7. The estimated cost of the proposed bolt = INR 3.95
8. The estimated cost saved = 20.72 - 3.95 = INR 16.77
Proposal: Replace Brass with Steel in the manufacture of the Main Terminal Connector Bolt within the Engine-Bay Fusebox Assembly of MGI

Cost Reduction Idea: Replace the Black Coated tracks used in the window regulators with Galvanized Steel tracks
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing,
1. Per Sq.M rate for black coating - INR 80 (assumed)
2. Per Sq.M rate for zinc coating - INR 55 (assumed)
3. Coating Area of the Double Track Drum and Pulley System (single track) - 78000mm2 
4. Coating Area of the Single Track Drum and Pulley System (single track) - 61600mm2
5. The cost of coating the Double Track Drum and Pulley System with black coating = 6.24*4 (front LH&RH) = INR 24.96
6. The cost of coating the Single Track Drum and Pulley System with black coating = 4.88*2 (rear LH&RH) = INR 9.76
7. The cost of coating the Double Track Drum and Pulley System with zinc coating = 4.29*4 (front LH&RH) = INR 17.16
8. The cost of coating the Single Track Drum and Pulley System with zinc coating = 3.35*2 (front LH&RH) = INR 6.7

9. The estimated cost saved at both front and rear ends = (24.96 + 9.76) - (17.16 + 6.7) = 34.72 - 23.86 = INR 10.86
Proposal: Replace the Black Coated tracks used in the window regulators with Galvanized Steel tracks

Cost Reduction Idea: Replace the oxygen sensors used in MGI with the oxygen sensors used in KIA
Way Forward: Homeroom ideas to be bunched up and taken up for implementation at program level
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing,
1. The cost of the current oxygen sensors - INR 897.19
2. The current weight - 100g
3. The cost of the proposed oxygen sensors - INR 785.22
4. The estimated weight - 91g

5. The estimated cost saved on a pair = 897.19 - 785.22 = 111.97*2 = (223.94 - 15% tolerance) = INR 190.34
6. The estimated weight saved on a pair = 100 - 91 = 9*2 = 18g
Proposal: Replace the oxygen sensors used in MGI with the oxygen sensors used in KIA
Imp: Homeroom ideas to be bunched up and taken up for implementation at program level

Cost Reduction Idea: Replace PMMA+ASA with PC-ABS in the manufacture of the LH and RH Side Spoilers
Estimated Cost Savings:
The evaluation is done before teardown, the values will change after the costing,
1. Per Kg rate of PMMA+ASA = INR 400 (assumed)
2. Per Kg rate of PC-ABS = INR 350 (assumed)
3. The current weight of the trim - 111.8g
4. The estimated weight of the proposed trim - 107.32g
5. The estimated weight savings on both the LH and RH ends = 111.8 - 107.32 = 4.48*2 = 8.96g
6. The current cost of the trim = INR 44.72
7. The estimated cost of the proposed trim = INR 37.56
8. The estimated cost savings on both the LH and RH ends = 44.72 - 37.56 = 7.16*2 = INR 14.32
Proposal: Replace PMMA+ASA with PC-ABS in the manufacture of the LH and RH Side Spoilers
